def: s.f. pl. (of shārěʻ), High roads.

ety: v.n. fr. شول 'to rise,' &c.
def: s.m. The tenth month of the Mohammadan year (the month next after Ramazān; — on the first day of Shawwāl the festival of Bairām or ʻId is celebrated, and it is customary to make presents, &c.).

ety: Pārsī shāyaṭ; 3rd pers. sing. aor. of shāyistan, rt. shāy; see shāyista
def: adv. It may be, perhaps, perchance, possibly, probably
subs: It may be that it is, it may be so, perhaps it is.

ety: imperf. part. of shāyistan; see shāyista
def: adj. Proper, fit, suitable; worthy; desirable; agreeable; lawful, legal, allowed, permitted; possible; contingent
subs: (-ko), To be proper (for), be suitable or fitting (to), to suit, become, befit.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شایان (šāyān).

def: s.m. pl. (of shait̤ān), Devils, demons.

ety: perf. part. of shāyistan, fr. shāy = shāh˚ = Zend khshay˚, rt. khshi = S. क्षि+stan = hastan
def: adj. Worthy, honourable; fitting, proper, suitable; decent; useful; expedient; lawful, unobjectionable; affable, well-bred, courteous, polite; gentle, docile, meek, quiet

ety: abst. s. fr. shāyista, q.v.
def: s.f. Suitableness, propriety, fitness, aptitude; consistency; conveniency, expediency; affability, urbanity, politeness, courtesy

ety: act. part of شذّ 'to be or become apart,' &c.
def: adj. Uncommon, unusual, rare, extraordinary, exceptional, abnormal, irregular, anomalous; wonderful, miraculous; — adv. Rarely, seldom

ety: v.n. fr. شعر 'to know,' &c.
def: s.m. Mark, signal, sign, countersign, password, parole; habit, custom, practice; method, manner; a garment worn next to the body, an under-garment (opp. to dis̤ār); — (shěʻār is much used in comp. to form adj., e.g. jādū-shěʻār, 'magical'; — karam-shěʻār, 'adorned with, or distinguished for, generosity').
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شعار (ši'ār), from Arabic شِعَار (šiʕār).

ety: fr. shěʻār
def: adj. Of or relating to custom, &c.; customary, habitual.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شعاری (ši'ārī), derived from Arabic شِعَار (šiʕār).

def: (v.n. fr. شعب 'to collect, or draw together,' &c.), s.m.(?) A road in a mountain, a pass, defile; a ravine; a depressed tract, a hollow in the ground.

ety: said to be a contrac. of nasheb; but cf. شعب sheʻb
def: s.m. A descent, declivity; a hollow; the lower part or bottom (of a mountain, &c.); — adv. At the bottom (of), under, below.

ety: Pehl. shet; Zend khshaêta, fr. khshiṭ, rt. khshi; S. क्षित्, rt. क्षि (क्षयति)
def: adj. & s.m. Bright, shining, clear; — light; the sun; fascination (cf. ḵẖẉurshed).

ety: perf. part. of sheftan, another form of shuftan; rt. Zend khshub = S. क्षुभ्
def: part. & s.m. Mad; distracted (with love), infatuated, enamoured (of, -kā); — a distracted or infatuated person, a lover
subs: s.m. The smooth red peach; an apricot (cf. shaft-rang)
(-kā), To be mad (upon, or after), to be enamoured (of), be desperately in love (with).

ety: pl. of sheḵẖ
def: s.m. (dialec.), Shaikhs, &c.
subs: s.m. A town inhabited by Shaikhs or descendants of the prophet.

ety: fr. shaiḵẖ
def: s.f. Boasting, bragging; boast
subs: and sheḵẖī jatāna = sheḵẖī mārnā, q.v.
(-kī), The boasting to be taken out (of); to have (one's) pride humbled.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَیْخِی (šayxī).

ety: shaiḵẖ+ṛā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. Son of a shekh (used in contempt).

ety: inf. n. of شعر 'to know'; 'to speak in verse'
def: s.m. Poetry, verse; a verse, a couplet
subs: s.f. The art of poetry; writing poetry.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic شِعْر (šiʕr).

ety: prob. akin to S. शॄ
def: s.m. 'Lion of the water'; an alligator
subs: s.m. A tiger's (or a lion's) cub; — a small gun
s.f. 'The lion and the kid'; a boy's game
s.m. 'The lion of God,' a title of 'Alī, and of Ḥusain
s.m. 'A tiger in effigy'; a person only fit to be looked at; a braggart
v.n. 'To utter the cry of a tiger'; to roar; to vomit with a loud noise
s.m. 'The lion of the sky,' the sun; the sign Leo
adj. & s.m. lit. 'Lion-man'; brave, valorous; — a brave man; (in the language of the devout) one who, by the practice of religious austerities, has obtained peculiar eminence in spiritual matters
v.n. 'To be the tiger'; to get the better (of), to overcome; be superior (to), be stronger; to tyrannize (over), to oppress.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شیر (šēr).

def: adj. & adv. Tiger-like; fierce as a tiger or lion; — in the manner of a tiger or lion.

ety: fr. shěʻr
def: adj. Relating to poetry, poetic, poetical.

ety: fr. sher
def: adj. Of or relating to a tiger or lion; lion-like; fierce; bold; — s.f. Lion-like conduct; fierceness; boldness, intrepidity.

ety: sher, q.v.+S. aff. इनी
def: s.f. A tigress; a lioness.

ety: perhaps akin to S. शिल्प
def: s.m. Business, trade, profession; manner, habit, custom, practice; amorous ways and looks (syn. ćāl; rawish; t̤arz)
subs: adj. & s.m. Expert, skilful, clever; — an artist, artisan; a tradesman.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شیوَه (šēwa)

def: s.m. Grief, mourning, lamentation.

ety: for A. شيعة shīʻat, v.n. fr. شيع 'to follow,' &c.
def: s.m. A particular party or sect who follow ʻAlī (affirming that he was the rightful Imām after Moḥammad); a follower of the sect of 'Alī; a sectary (opp. to sunnī).
s.m. A span, the space between the extremity of the thumb and that of the little finger when extended apart.

ety: pl. of shadīd
def: adj. Strong, violent, vehement; — s.m. Name of an impious Eastern monarch who arrogated divine power to himself.

ety: for A. شدة, inf. n. of شد 'to be distressing,' &c.
def: s.f. Hardness, firmness; strength, power, force; vehemence, violence, intenseness, stress, pressure, severity, rigour; difficulty, hardship, adversity, trial, trouble, distress, affliction
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شدت (šiddat), from Arabic شِدَّة (šidda).

ety: inf. n. of شفو 'to restore to health,' &c.
def: s.f. Recovery (from sickness), healing, convalescence; a medicine, remedy, cure
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِفَاء (šifā'), from Arabic شِفَاء (šifāʔ).

ety: another form of shuguft, q.v.
def: s.m. Split, rent, slit, crack, cleft, fissure, chink, crevice, chasm, flaw; — act. part. (of shigāftan), Splitting, rending, cracking, &c. (used as last member of comp.).

ety: = shagāl, q.v.
def: s.m. A jackal.

ety: cf. shikār
def: part. adj. Eating, devouring (used as last member of compounds); — s.m. Anything eatable.

ety: rt. Z. khshǒiwra = S. क्षिप्र rt. क्षिप्
def: adj. Rare, fine; great, good; of great retinue and state; powerful; venerable, respectable; glorious, beautiful, excellent; vehement, strong, vigorous; thick, stout.

ety: or shikift; rt. Zend khshub = S. क्षुभ्
def: s.m. Wonder, astonishment.

ety: see shiguftan
def: s.m. A bud, blossom; flower; (colloq.) a fabrication; — a squib
subs: To bud, blossom; to put forth young shoots; — to produce something new and wonderful.

ety: or shikufta; perf. part. of shiʻguftan, q.v.
def: part. Expanded, blown (as a flower); blooming; flourishing
subs: s.f. Blooming or expansion of mind, delight
v.n. To be blown (a flower), to bloom; to flourish; to be delighted.

ety: abst. s. fr. shigufta
def: s.f. Expanding of a flower, blooming; bloom; beauty; flourishing state or condition; expansion (of the heart), delight, pleasure; astonishment.

ety: orig. shikuftan, &c.; rt. Zend khshub = S. क्षुभ्
def: inf. & gerund, To blossom, bloom; blooming, &c.
s.m. Neighing, neigh (of a horse).

ety: v.n. fr. شہب 'to be or become gray,' &c.
def: s.m. Flame (of fire); a firebrand; a brightly-shining star; a shooting or falling star; a meteor.
s.m. pl. (of shajjat = shajj, q.v.), Flesh-wounds (rare). s.m. A wound or personal injury short of destroying life (rare).
s.m. = shigāf, q.v. s.m. Split, rent, slit, crack, cleft, fissure, chink, crevice, chasm, flaw; — act. part. (of shigāftan), Splitting, rending, cracking, &c. (used as last member of comp.).
s.m. The belly, stomach
subs: s.m. A slave to his belly, an epicure, a glutton; a servant who works for his board
or shikam-parwar, s.m. A pamperer of his belly, an epicure, a glutton; a sensualist
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِکَم (šikam), from Middle Persian 𐭠𐭱𐭪𐭬𐭡 (aškamb).

ety: fr. شکنب = shikam, q.v.
def: s.m. Paunch, tripe.

adj. Of or relating to the belly; big-bellied; included; private, secret; subordinate, dependent; sub-tenanted; — s.m. A big-bellied man, a glutton; — skin of the belly; — a peasant who cultivates land belonging to another; a sub-tenant

ety: rt. of shikastan; see shikast
def: part. & s.m. Breaking, crushing, overthrowing, routing; — breaker, &c. (used in comp., e.g. ʻahd-shikan, s.m. 'promise-breaker'); — s.f. Shrinking, shrivelling, constringing; — a curl; a ply, fold, plait; a furrow, crease, wrinkle, &c.
subs: (-meṅ), To be creased, be wrinkled, be crumpled, &c.

ety: abst. of shikan, q.v.
def: s.f. Breaking, breach, destruction, &c. (used in comp., e.g. ʻahd-shikanī, 'breach of agreement or promise').

ety: fr. shikan, q.v.
def: s.m. A bookbinder's press; a clamp; stocks (for the legs); pain, torture, torment, rack
subs: (-ko), To put in a press; to rack, to torture.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِکَنْجَه (šikanja).

ety: prob. shi = Zend uz or uś = S. उद्+kār, rt. Zend kar = S. कर् (कृ, कॄ); or Zend kareṭ = S. कर्त् (कृत)
def: s.m. Hunting, the chase; prey, game; plunder, booty, pillage, spoil; — perquisites
subs: s.m. Cords, &c. for tying game in (or to fasten it to the saddle)
adj. & s.m. Whose trade or business is hunting; — a professional hunter
v.t. To hunt, take, catch as prey; to prey upon; to bring into one's clutches
(-kā), To hunt, to go a-hunting; to catch, take (fish, &c., e.g. maćhliyoṅ-kā shikār khelnā)
s.f. Hunting-ground, chase; preserve for game
To kill game; to hunt, &c. ( = shikār karnā). — shikār-ě-māhī, s.f. Fishing; — a tax on fishing
v.n. To be hunted; to fall a prey, become the prey (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِکَار (šikār), possibly borrowed from Sogdian 𐽁𐼸𐼰𐽀 (škʾr), from Proto-Iranian *skar-.

ety: fr. skikardan; see shikār
def: s.m. A hawk, a falcon; the sparrow-hawk.
adj. Of or belonging to the chase, relating to hunting; of prey; hunting-; — a hunter, sportsman, a fowler, an angler
subs: s.m. A bird or beast of prey; game
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِکَارِی (šikārī).

ety: v.n. fr. shikastan; rt. Zend śkeñd (śéid, śćind) = S. छिद्
def: s.f. Breaking, breakage, fracture; a breach; defeat, rout; deficiency, loss, damage; — adj. (contrac. of shikasta), Broken; odd, uneven, unpaired
subs: part. adj. Broken off, interrupted; defeated
or shikast-o-reḵẖt, s.f. Injuries, damages, dilapidations; repairs (necessary for a house)
and shikast honā, v.n. To suffer defeat, to be defeated, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِکَسْت (šikast), from شِکَسْتَن (šikastan), from Middle Persian škstn' (škastan, “to break”).

ety: perf. part. of shikastan; see shikast
def: part. Broken; defeated, routed; carried away (by inundation, as river-banks, &c.); reduced to straits; bankrupt; sick; wounded; weak, infirm; — s.m. 'Broken writing,' the Persian running hand
subs: adj. 'Broken-winged'; broken down, reduced to straits, distressed, afflicted, wretched
adj. 'Having the legs broken'; infirm, broken down, reduced
adj. Broken-down, in bad plight, distressed, indigent, wretched, ruined
s.f. Bad condition or circumstances, reverse of fortune, indigence, distress, wretchedness
adj. Distressed in mind, afflicted; grieved, offended
adj. & s.m. By whom engagements are broken, faithless; — one who breaks his engagements, &c.

ety: abst. s. fr. next
def: s.f. The act of breaking; breakage, fracture, rupture; breach, violation; brokenness; defeat, rout, discomfiture; infirmity, sickness; grief, sadness, dejection, affliction.

ety: for A. شکاية, inf. n. of شکو 'to complain,' &c.
def: s.f. Complaint, accusation; lamentation, moaning; ailment
subs: (-kī), To remove a cause of complaint
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِکایَت (šikāyat), from Arabic شِكَايَة (šikāya).

ety: fr. shikāyat
def: adj. Of complaint, complaining; plaintive.

s.m. & adj. See s.v. shiqq. s.f. The half (of a thing); the side (of a mountain, &c.); the counterpart (of a thing); a brother; a friend; — a large division of a country, a tract of country forming a collectorate; — kind, sort; difficulty, hardship, distress, affliction, trouble, labour, inconvenience; suspense; weariness, languor (syn. mashaqqat)
subs: s.m. An officer appointed to collect the revenue from a shiq; — adj. Difficult, distressing; uncertain, perplexing, &c.
s.f. The office or business of a shiq-dār; — uncertainty, perplexity, &c.

ety: akin to shigift, q.v.
def: s.f. Patience, long-suffering.

ety: imperf. part. of shikebīdan, fr. shikeb, q.v.
def: adj. Patient, long-suffering.
s.f. Patience, exercising patience; long-suffering; toleration.
s.m. = shigift, q.v. s.m. Wonder, astonishment.
(v.n. fr. شكّ 'to stick, cleave,' &c.), s.m.(?) A covering that is put upon the backs of the two curved extremities of the bow; — an upper garment.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَک (šak), borrowed from Arabic شَكّ (šakk).

ety: v.n. fr. شقّ; see shaqq
def: s.f. The half (of a thing); the side (of a mountain, &c.); the counterpart (of a thing); a brother; a friend; — a large division of a country, a tract of country forming a collectorate; — kind, sort; difficulty, hardship, distress, affliction, trouble, labour, inconvenience; suspense; weariness, languor (syn. mashaqqat)
subs: s.m. An officer appointed to collect the revenue from a shiq; — adj. Difficult, distressing; uncertain, perplexing, &c.
s.f. The office or business of a shiq-dār; — uncertainty, perplexity, &c.

def: s.m. = shugūfa, q.v.
part. = shigufta, q.v. part. Expanded, blown (as a flower); blooming; flourishing
subs: s.f. Blooming or expansion of mind, delight
v.n. To be blown (a flower), to bloom; to flourish; to be delighted.

ety: = S. शल
def: s.m. A short spear, a javelin; a trident; a harpoon.
s.m.(?) The wood-apple (syn. bel); — a sort of quince.
s.m. A tablecloth.
s.m.(?) A dispute (with one) upon false grounds; contention, quarrel, litigation; tumult.
adj. Quarrelling without reason; contentious, quarrelsome.

ety: acc. of shimāl
def: adv. To the north, northward.
adj. Of or relating to the quarter of the north, northern.
s.m. Name of one of Yazīd's generals who slew Husain in the plains of Karbalā; — (hence) adj. Merciless, wicked, vile, infamous.
s.f. (dialec.) = sīnī, q.v. s.f. A salver, a plate, tray, trough (syn. qāb; thālī).

r: shīn, śa, sha, sh, Abjad, shimāl, Shaʻbān, śa-kār
ety: = Hindī श śa; and ष sha
def: s.m. The letter or sound ś
subs: s.m. The letter or sound sha.
wety: From Classical Persian ـش (-iš).

ety: rt. Zend śnu; S. स्नु (स्नौति)
def: s.m.(?) Swimming.
s.m. Swimming ( = shinā, q.v.). s.m.(?) Swimming.

ety: fr. shinās
def: s.f. Knowing, &c. (used as last member of compounds).

ety: fr. shinūdan, or shunūdan, or shanīdan; rt. Zend śru = S. श्रु
def: part. & s.m. Hearing; — hearer.

ety: fr. shinūdan, or shunūdan, or shanīdan; rt. Zend śru = S. श्रु
def: part. & s.m. Hearing; — hearer.

ety: fr. shinā, q.v.
def: s.m. A swimmer.
s.f. The act of swimming.

ety: Zend khshīra; S. क्षीर
def: s.m. Milk (syn. dūdh) — shīr-birinj, s.m. Rice boiled in milk (syn. khīr)
subs: s.f. A substance which rests like dew on certain trees and is used in medicine; manna
s.m. A suckling, an infant, a babe
s.m. 'Milk and sugar'; affection; close intimacy; intimate friends; — a kind of silk cloth
s.m. A fine plump suckling, a fatling
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شیر (šēr).

ety: also شري shirā, inf. n. of شري 'to give (a thing
def: s.m. A sale; a purchase; traffic.

ety: fr. shīr, q.v.
def: s.m. Juice (of fruit); sap; syrup; — new wine, must
subs: s.m. Sap of existence, essence of life.

ety: for A. شراکة, v.n. fr. شرك 'to share,' &c.
def: s.f. Sharing, participation, partnership, fellowship; combination, confederacy, alliance, league; a corporation, coparcenary, joint-stock company; land, &c. held in common; community of interests (syn. shirkat, q.v.)
subs: v.n. To participate (in); to form a partnership (with); to combine, to league (with), &c.

def: s.m. The stitching of the back of a book; sewing button-holes
subs: s.f. The binding of a book.

ety: fr. shīr
def: adj. Milky; relating to milk; — s.f. Part of a ship.

ety: shīr, q.v.+īn = Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Sweet; pleasant; gentle, affable
subs: adj. Of sweet or pleasing manners
s.f. Sweetness of speech, affability; eloquence
adj. Of sweet or gentle manners; of mild disposition
or shīrīn-guftār, or shīrīn-maqāl, adj. = shīrīn-zabān, q.v.

def: s.f. Sweetmeat, sweets; an offering of sweetmeats; — eloquence.

ety: inf. n. of شرك 'to share,' &c.
def: s.m. Partnership, copartnership, a company, society; — (v.n. fr. اشرك, iv. of شرك) 'the attribution of a copartner to God,' belief in a plurality of gods, polytheism, paganism, unbelief, infidelity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شرک (širk), from Arabic شِرْك (širk).

ety: for A. شرکة, inf. n. of شرك 'to share,' &c.
def: s.f. Partnership, copartnership, &c. ( = shirākat, q.v.). s.f. Sharing, participation, partnership, fellowship; combination, confederacy, alliance, league; a corporation, coparcenary, joint-stock company; land, &c. held in common; community of interests (syn. shirkat, q.v.)
subs: v.n. To participate (in); to form a partnership (with); to combine, to league (with), &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِرْکَت (šerkat), from Arabic شِرْكَة (širka).

ety: rel. n. fr. shirkat
def: adj. Common, in common, held in partnership, &c.
s.f. contrac. of shīrīnī, q.v.
s.f. An artery (cf. S. śirā).
s.m. contrac. (in comp.) of shīsha, q.v. (e.g. shīsh-maḥal, q.v.s.v. shīsha).

ety: = S. सिक्ष्य
def: s.m. Glass; glass-ware; a glass bottle; a looking-glass, mirror, pier-glass, &c.; a pane of glass
subs: (-ko), To show a looking-glass (the practice of barbers, with the view of ascertaining whether their services are required)
s.f. The present given to a barber for showing a looking-glass

vulg. shīsh-maḥal, s.m. A house or palace all hung with glass, an apartment or a cabinet adorned with mirrors; an apartment of which the walls are inlaid with pieces of glass; a glass house, a crystal-palace
s.m. 'The devil of the bottle'; gin, &c.
'To swell out like glass'; to swell with pride
v.t. 'To bring down (and shut up an evil spirit) into a bottle,' to subdue and confine (an evil spirit) by magic; to captivate, charm; to assuage, soothe, appease.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شیشه (šīša), from Middle Persian. First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi شیشہ (śyśh).

ety: dim. fr. shīsha
def: s.f. A small glass; a small bottle, a phial
subs: (-ko), To make one smell hartshorn or smelling-salts; to administer chloroform, &c.
wety: Diminutive of شیشہ (šīša), from Classical Persian شیشه (šīša).

ety: v.n. fr. شتو
def: s.m. Winter.

ety: shi+tāb, rt. of tāftan or tāb-īdan = Zend uś or uz+rt. tap = S. उद्+तप्
def: s.m. Haste, &c. ( = shitābī, q.v.); adj. & adv. Hasty, quick, speedy; — quickly, expeditiously, soon, directly
subs: adj. & s.m. Hasty, quick, expeditious; — an active or expeditious person

ety: imperf. part. of shitāb-īdan; see shitāb
def: adj. Hastening, making great haste, hurrying.

ety: shitāb, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Quickness, haste, expedition, despatch; celerity, speed; — adv. Quickly, hastily, &c. ( = shitāb).
s.m. The ankle.
s.m. Neighing, neigh (of a horse).
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to the sect of ʻAlī; — a follower of the sect of 'Alī ( = shīʻa).

ety: imperat. & rt. of shustan; fr. Zend śud = S. शुध्
def: part. adj. & s.m. Washing; — washer (used as last member of compounds).
wety: Borrowed from English show.

ety: v.n. of شعّ 'to be scattered or dispersed'
def: s.f. The rays or beams of the sun, sunbeams, sunshine, radiance, light, splendour, lustre.
adj. Of or relating to the sun's rays, full of rays, radial, radiant.

ety: fr. shustan; see sho or shū
def: s.m. Washing; a wash.

ety: for A. شعبة shǒʻbat, v.n. fr. شعب 'to collect;' 'to separate,' &c.
def: s.m. A crack, cleft in a mountain; a watercourse, a channel in which water flows; a branch; a ramification, a division.

ety: for A. شعبذة shaʻbaẕat, inf. n. of شعبذ; or شعوذة, inf. n. of شعوذ, 'to practise legerdemain'
def: s.m. Legerdemain, sleight of hand, juggling, conjuration
subs: s.m. One who practises legerdemain, a juggler, conjurer
s.f. Sleight of hand, conjuring, juggling trick, jugglery, hocus-pocus.

ety: for A. شعبذة shaʻbaẕat, inf. n. of شعبذ; or شعوذة, inf. n. of شعوذ, 'to practise legerdemain'
def: s.m. Legerdemain, sleight of hand, juggling, conjuration
subs: s.m. One who practises legerdemain, a juggler, conjurer
s.f. Sleight of hand, conjuring, juggling trick, jugglery, hocus-pocus.

ety: rt. सिध्
def: part. adj. Accomplished, effected, brought about, completed, finished, perfected, consummated; performed, fulfilled; realized; obtained, acquired, gained; successful; settled, established, substantiated, demonstrated, proved; admitted to be true or right; valid, sound, good; judged, determined, adjudicated, decided, terminated (as a law-suit, &c.); paid, liquidated, discharged, settled (as a debt, &c.); framed, formed, enacted, made (as a regulation, or an award, &c.); ready (as money, &c.), thoroughly prepared; made up, concocted, compounded (as drugs, &c.); cooked, dressed; matured, ripened, ripe; brought into subjection (by magical power); thoroughly skilled or versed (in science, or in magical arts, &c.); endowed with supernatural powers; perfected or sanctified (by penance, or austerities, &c.); emancipated, beatified; sacred, holy, pious, divine; immortal, eternal; well-known, eminent, celebrated; illustrious, shining, splendid; — s.m. A semi-divine being supposed to be of great purity and holiness, and said to be specially characterized by the eight siddhis or supernatural faculties (the Siddhas, together with the Vidyādharas, Munis, &c. are believed to inhabit the Bhuvarlok or middle region between the earth and the sun); — an inspired sage, a seer; a holy personage, a great saint, one who has subjected his will to the eight siddhis; — an ascetic who by mystical and austere practices has effected one or all of five purposes, viz. 1˚ the influence or wealth of the gods; 2˚ the form of the gods; 3˚ the society of the gods; 4˚ residence in the divine lokas; 5˚ identification with a deity; — an adept in magical or mystical arts, a magician, one who by the performance of certain mystical rites has acquired certain superhuman powers; — the twenty-first of the astronomical periods called yogas
subs: (˚dha+ar˚), adj. & s.m. Having attained (one's) aim, successful; — one who has accomplished his aim or object; — white mustard

(˚dha + an˚), s.m. Demonstrated conclusion, establisḥed truth, result, consequence, the determination or result of investigation or discussion, conclusion, decision; reliable doctrine; a system of science (esp. of astronomy, geometry, &c.), a common name of several treatises upon astronomy, astrology, algebra, and other sciences
s.m. The logically correct side of an argument
s.f. The point in an argument which is regarded as a logical conclusion

adj. Appertaining to a logical demonstration; — s.m. One who establishes or proves his conclusions, a logician; demonstrator; one learned in scientific text-books; a follower of the Mīmānsā philosophy

s.m. A perfect or completely sanctified personage, one who by abstraction and austerities has attained that degree of divine perfection which enables him to accomplish anything he desires; a sage, seer; an adept in mystical rites
adj. & s.m. Having perfected metallic fluids, mineral, metallic; — mercury, quicksilver
s.m. The performance of magical or mystical rites (for the acquisition of supernatural powers, &c.); the materials employed in mystical or chemical processes
s.m. An observer of the laws
(-ko), To carry into effect, to obey
v.t. To accomplish, effect; to complete, finish; to perfect, to sanctify; to bring under subjection by magic; &c.; — v.n. To make ready, to set out, to start (cf. sidhārnā)
(˚dha+un˚), adj. Having the face set to business, fit or ready for business, &c.
v.n. To be accomplished; to be perfect, &c. (see siddh).

ety: siddh, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.t. To exercise or to accomplish siddhi, q.v.
= S سدهانتي सिद्धान्ती siddhāntī, See s.v. siddha or siddh.
= S سدهانت सिद्धान्त siddhānt, See s.v. siddha or siddh.
s.f. Accomplishment, completion, perfection; success; validity (of a rule, &c.).

s.f. Accomplishment, fulfilment, consummation, completion, perfection, complete attainment (of any object), performance; success, prosperity; — settlement, establishment, substantiation, demonstration, proof, indisputable conclusion or position; validity (of a rule or law); — certainty, truth, accuracy, correctness; decision, adjudication, determination (of a lawsuit); payment, liquidation (of a debt), discharge, acquittance; — solution (of a problem); preparation, cooking, maturing, maturity; readiness; complete knowledge; understanding, intellect; complete perfection or sanctification (by penance or austerities); final emancipation, supreme felicity, beatitude; the acquisition of supernatural powers by magical means or through the performance of certain mystical or alchymical rites or processes; the supernatural faculty so acquired (eight are usually enumerated, viz. animan, mahiman, laghiman, gariman, prāpti, prākāmya, īśi-tva, vaśi-tva, qq.v.)
subs: any marvellous skill or capability; concealment

vanishing, making oneself invisible; a magical shoe or slipper (supposed to have the power of conveying the wearer wherever he likes); — good effect or result, use, advantage; — name of a certain yoga (either the sixteenth of the astronomical periods or the nineteenth of the twenty-eight astrological yogas); — the hemp-plant, Cannabis sativa
s.m. Giver of perfection, &c.; conferrer of felicity or beatitude
s.f. (in Gram.) The junction of a consonant with a vowel
s.m. (f. -ī), One who possesses siddhi, q.v.

ety: S. सिद्धं; or सिद्धिः
def: s.f. Straightness, directness; direct line, direction, aim (cf. sūdh); — adj. = sīdhā, q.v.
subs: (-kī), To take the line (of); to take aim (at); to go in a direct line (to); to lay out a line (of road)
adv. In a straight line (with, -kī), in the direction (of).

ety: Prk. सिद्धओ; S. सिद्ध+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Straight, direct, even; to the point; close (as a translation); point-blank; opposite, facing; erect, upright; accurate, correct, right, proper, excellent; plain, simple, artless; easy (to be accomplished, &c.); straightforward, honest, fair, candid; quiet, gentle, tractable; favourable, kind; right (hand); auspicious, lucky; — adv. Directly, straight, &c.; — s.m. Undressed victuals, provisions; forage; a settled allowance; — a charge made for provisions or supplies (to travellers, &c.); — sīdhā banānā (-ko), To make straight, to straighten; to set right, to regulate, to correct; to tame, train, teach( = sīdhā karnā)
subs: v.t. To straighten; to smooth, plane; to rectify, to adjust, correct, set right, put to rights, make proper; to chastise; to tame, train, teach; — to level (a gun); to take aim
v.n. To be or become straight, to come right, to be on the right path; to be rectified or corrected; to be chastised; to be tamed, be trained
To deviate from the straight path, to go astray
s.f. A plain and simple style
adv. Naturally; in a straightforward manner, in all honesty
(-ko), To speak plainly (to), to rate (one) soundly; to abuse roundly or in set terms
adv. In a direct or straightforward manner, fairly, honestly; smoothly, softly, quietly; civilly, respectfully.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀺𑀤𑁆𑀥 (siddha), from Sanskrit सिद्ध (siddha).

def: s.f. = H سيدهاپن सीधापन sīdhā-pan, s.m. (for ˚pan, and āʼī, see sīṭhā-pan, &c.), Straightness; simplicity, artlessness, straightforwardness, genuineness, candour, honesty, uprightness, rectitude; inoffensiveness, harmlessness, quietness, tractableness.
s.f. Straightness, directness; straightforwardness, frankness, candour, artlessness, simplicity; goodness, excellence.
v.n. = sidhārnā, q.v. v.n. To set out, set off, start; to go, depart; to depart this life, to die (the word is, generally speaking, one of affection or tenderness).

s.m. = H سيدهائي सीधाई sīdhāʼī, s.f. (for ˚pan, and āʼī, see sīṭhā-pan, &c.), Straightness; simplicity, artlessness, straightforwardness, genuineness, candour, honesty, uprightness, rectitude; inoffensiveness, harmlessness, quietness, tractableness.

ety: fr. S. सिध्+कार
def: v.n. To set out, set off, start; to go, depart; to depart this life, to die (the word is, generally speaking, one of affection or tenderness).
wety: Derived from سدھار (sudhār), which is from Sanskrit *शुद्धकार (*śuddhakāra, “the act of purifying or removing errors”), from शुद्ध (śuddha) + कार (kāra) (compare Sanskrit शुद्धीकरोति (śuddhīkaroti), from शुद्धीकृ (śuddhīkṛ, “to purify, to remove errors”, root), from शुद्ध (śuddha, adjective) + ईकृ (īkṛ, root form)). Cognate with Punjabi ਸੁਧਾਰਨਾ (sudhārnā) / سُدھارنا (sudhārnā), Nepali सुधार्नु (sudhārnu), Bengali …

def: v.n. = sidhārnā, q.v. v.n. To set out, set off, start; to go, depart; to depart this life, to die (the word is, generally speaking, one of affection or tenderness).

ety: 2nd pers. pl. imperat. of sidhārnā
def: intj. Be off! off! away!

ety: sidh, q.v.+Prk. अउल्ली = S. क+उल+ई
def: s.f. Longing of a pregnant woman.
s.f. Straightness, directness; straightforwardness, frankness, candour, artlessness, simplicity; goodness, excellence.
s.f. = sīdh; and sīdhāʼī, qq.v. s.f. Straightness, directness; direct line, direction, aim (cf. sūdh); — adj. = sīdhā, q.v.
subs: (-kī), To take the line (of); to take aim (at); to go in a direct line (to); to lay out a line (of road)
adv. In a straight line (with, -kī), in the direction (of).

ety: inf. n. of صدق 'to speak truth,' &c.
def: s.m. Truth, veracity; sincerity, candour
subs: s.m. Truth and falsehood; the true and the false.

ety: see sednā; and cf. sījnā
def: v.n. To suffer affliction, to be afflicted.

ety: for A. سدرة sidrat, q.v.
def: s.m. The lote-tree
subs: s.m. 'The bird of the lote-tree' (of the Seventh Heaven), a name of the angel Gabriel.

ety: v.n. of un. fr. سدر 'to be dazzled,' &c.
def: s.f. A species of lote-tree, Rhamnus spina christi or R. nabeca
subs: s.f. The lote-tree in the Seventh Heaven (beyond which neither angel nor prophet passes, and which shades the water and Paradise: it is supposed to be the mansion of the angel Gabriel).

def: s.m.(?) Sea-shore, shore, margin, bank (rare).

ety: for ispahān or isfahān, q.v.
def: s.m. Name of a city of Persia (formerly capital of that empire).
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Isfahān; — a native of Isfahān.

ety: prob. akin to P. sipār = Zend śpāra
def: s.m. Earthenware (esp. such as is glazed); a tile; a shard, potsherd
wety: From Classical Persian سفال (sufāl ~ sifāl, “earthenware”).

ety: prob. akin to P. sipār = Zend śpāra
def: s.m. Earthenware (esp. such as is glazed); a tile; a shard, potsherd
adj. Made of earth, earthen; made of earthenware.

ety: for A. سفانة, v.n. fr. سفن 'to pare and smooth,' &c.
def: s.f. The art, or craft, or occupation of ship-building, and of navigation.

ety: for A. سفارة, inf. n. of سفر 'to make peace (between
def: s.f. Making peace (between), pacifying, acting as a mediator or messenger of peace; — mediation; the office of a mediator or messenger of peace; composition, writing (of a book).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سِفَارَت (sifārat).

ety: for sipārish, fr. sipārdan; rt. Zend śpar = S. स्फुर्, स्फर्
def: s.f. Recommendation, introduction; intercession; interest
subs: (-kī), To recommend, to commend (to); to intercede (for)
s.m. A letter of recommendation or introduction (syn. sifārishī ćiṭṭhī or ḵẖaṭ).
ex: مَیں نے اَپنے دوسْت کِی سِفارِش پَر یہ کِتَاب پَڑِھی۔
ma͠i ne apne dost kī sifāriś par ye kitāb paṛhī.
I read this book upon my friend's recommendation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سِفارِش (sifāriš).

def: adj. & s.m. Recommendatory, of recommendation, of introduction; — a person recommended or introduced by another
subs: s.f. or sifārishī ḵẖat̤, s.m. Letter of recommendation or of introduction.

ety: for A. صفة, inf. n. of وصف 'to describe,' &c.
def: s.f. Description; a definition; (in Gram.) a descriptive epithet; a descriptive clause or sentence; a qualificative; an adjective; a quality, attribute, property; praise; form, mode, manner; — adj. Like, resembling (used as last member of compounds)
subs: An adjective with its substantive, the qualificative and the qualified.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صِفَت (sifat), from Arabic صِفَة (ṣifa).

def: s.f. pl. (of ṣifat, q.v.), Qualities, properties, attributes; good qualities.
adj. Accidental; artificial; extrinsic; acquired (applied to qualities not innate).

ety: for A. صقتية, fem. of صفتي, rel. n. fr. ṣifat
def: adj. Descriptive; qualificative, adjectival
subs: s.m. A descriptive or qualificative sentence.

ety: for A. سفلة siflat, v.n. fr. سفل; see sufl
def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Low, mean, ignoble, base, vile, sordid, contemptible; avaricious, miserly, stingy; envious; — a low or base man, &c.; the common people, the canaille
subs: s.m. Protector or patron of the ignoble
adj. Mean-spirited, of a base or ignoble disposition
s.m. A cup or plate to throw scraps, &c. on.

ety: abst. s. fr. next
def: s.f. Meanness, lowness, baseness.

ety: sifta+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. = siflagī, q.v. s.f. Meanness, lowness, baseness.

ety: v.n. fr. صفر 'to be empty,' &c.
def: s.m. A cypher. a dot, nought, zero.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صِفْر (sifr), from Arabic صِفْر (ṣifr), a semantic loan from Sanskrit शू॒न्य (śūnyá, “empty; void”), whence also سُونا (sūnā). Doublet of زِیرو (zīro).

def: adj. Thick, close, coarse (as cloth); hard, stiff; firm, strong (syn. dabīz).
(P. zift arabicized), s.m.(?) Soft pitch.

ety: for A. صيغة, v.n. fr. صوغ 'to cast, to mould,' &c.
def: s.m. A mould (for casting metal); make, form, fashion, mould; a word declinable and derivative; mood, tense, or voice (of a verb); conjugation; derivation; form (of process in law); a form of words used in (Mohammadan) marriage ceremonies, &c.; — trade, profession; section, department, office (e.g. sīg̠a-ě-ābkārī, Distillery department; ṣīg̠a-ě-māl, Revenue or finance department, &c.)
subs: v.t. To make one repeat the form of words prescribed in a marriage ceremony, to marry

def: s.m. (?), Thought, suspicion; enmity, hatred; calumny; word, speech, saying

ety: inf. n. of صغر 'to be small,' &c.
def: s.m. Smallness, minuteness.

ety: pl. of ṣag̠īr, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Small; — little people, the small
subs: The small and the great; the populace at large.

def: s.m. Thick cloth (see gāṛhā).

ety: S. शृङ्ग+युक्तकः
def: s.m. Hide, skin (with the horns attached).
adj. & adv. Ouick, speedy, swift, rapid; violent
subs: speedily, rapidly, swiftly; soon; — s.m. (in Astron.) Conjunction; parallax
s.m. (in Astron.) The distance from the conjunction (of a planet), commutation
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Going or moving quickly, swift, fleet, rapid, expeditious; — an expeditious person; a swift creature, a fleet animal.

def: s.f. Quickness, haste, speed, rapidity, fleetness; alertness, agility
s.m. = ṣaiqal-gar.
adj. (Braj), = sagrā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), All, entire, the whole, every, one and all, each and all.
s.m. (dialec.) Teak-wood ( = sāgaun).

ety: Prk. सीहो; S. सिंहः
def: s.m. A lion, &c. ( = siṅgh or siṅh).

ety: S. शल्लकी
def: s.m. A porcupine; a hedgehog ( = sāhī, q.v.). s.m. (old Hindī), corr. of shāh, q.v.: — 2˚ (dialec.), A religious mendicant, a Mohammadan faqīr ( = sāṅʼī).

ety: Zend thri; S. त्रि
def: adj. Three
subs: s.m. A militia soldier employed in collecting revenue, or in police duties; an establishment of peons; — s.f. Charges in the revenue accounts for the maintenance of the afore-mentioned soldiers and peons; a tribute, contingent; — three-monthly or quarterly payment; — si-bandī ughānā, To levy or collect three-monthly payments
adj. Three-fold, triple; three times, thrice
adj. & s.m. Having three boundaries; — a place where three boundaries meet
adj. Consisting of three letters, triliteral
adj. Having three harvests; yielding three crops
adv. Three times as much; a third time
adj. Three kinds, of three kinds
s.m. A village where the boundaries of three other villages meet; a village enclosed by the boundaries of three other villages; — si-gosha, adj. Three-cornered; triangular; — s.m. A triangle
adj. Having three stories or floors (a house, &c.).

def: s.m. pl. (of ṣāḥib, q.v.), Companions, &c.
s.f. corr. of siyāhī, q.v. s.f. Blackness, darkness; shade; a black spot; a black dye or tincture; ink; blacking; lamp-black (syn. kājal); (met.) a stigma, brand
s.m. pl. (of sahm, q.v.), Portions, shares, &c.

ety: also sehrā; — Prk. सेहरओ; S. शेखर+कः

subs: s.f. The brother-in-law's fee for fastening a chaplet on the bridegroom's head.

ety: fr. S. शिखर; see saharnā

ety: prob. fr. S. शिथिल

s.m. A species of black grain which grows in wheat-fields.
s.m. The shrub Trophis aspera ( = seʼoṛā, q.v.). s.m. The plant Trophis aspera.

ety: inf. n. of سحر 'to turn from (his or its
def: s.m. Enchantment, fascination, magic, sorcery (syn. jādū)
subs: s.m. Lawful magic; skilful eloquence; poetry
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَحَر (sahar), from Arabic سَحَر (saḥar).

ety: sihrā(nā)+āʼū = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: adj. Wearisome, tiresome, tedious.

ety: fr. siharnā
def: s.f. Shuddering, shivering, cold.

ety: S. सीहुण्डः
def: s.m. The milky hedge-plant, Euphorbia neriifolia, or E. antiquorum (syn. sihūṛ).

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَجْدَه (sajda), from Arabic سَجْدَة (sajda).

def: s.m. pl. (of sijda), Prostrations, bowings (in) prayer, adorations.

ety: caus. of sījhnā

ety: sīj˚ = Prk. सिज्ज(इ) = S. श्रीय(ते), passive of rt. श्री (or श्रा); — Prk. सिज्ज(इ), 'to exude,' &c., also = S. स्विद्य(ति), rt. स्विद्
def: v.n. To boil, seethe; to become soft or sodden (by boiling); to dissolve, melt away; to sweat, exude; to filtrate, percolate; — to be liquidated (a debt); to be received (as money)

def: adj. Well-arranged; neat, tidy; good, right, proper; pure, fine, choice; clean, polished, furbished

def: s.m. Written statement or contract; written attestation of a notary; register; the record or decree of a Qāẓī or judge, judicial record; seal of a judge (sigillum); — the recording angel.

def: adj. & s.m. Trusty, trustworthy; gentle, courteous; — a trusty person or friend; a gentleman.

ety: for A. ثقة s̤iqat, fem.; inf. n. of وثق
def: s.m. Trusting, confiding (in); confidence; — adj. & s.m. Trusty, worthy of confidence; — a trusty person, a confidant.

s.f. A chain ( = sāṅkal, q.v.). s.f. A chain, &c. ( = sāṅkar, q.v.). s.f. A chain; a door-chain; staple (of a lock); fetter; manacle; a kind of ornament worn by women, a twisted chain worn on the ankle.
